Structure generated by Swiss Model

Homology Modeling

Homology Modeling
Need 3-structures for understanding protein function, drug design
Problem:
• Many more sequences than 3-D structures
• De-novo prediction still difficult 
Becoming more possible:  I-Tasser: 
https://zhanglab.ccmb.med.umich.edu/I-TASSER/
Better solution for now and mid-term:
• Number of possible folds in nature appears to be limited
• Most proteins are related to a protein with known structure
➙Predict structure of the desired protein on basis of known protein

Five steps to homology modeling
1)
Identify structural templates
2)
Align target sequence and template structure
3)
Build the model
4)
Evaluate the quality of the model
5)
Repeat 2-4 (or 1-4) as needed
Alignment can be particularly challenging!
https://swissmodel.expasy.org/docs/help

Swiss-model
http://swissmodel.expasy.org/
II. De-novo generated models
3 modes of using Swiss-model to generate new homology models
• Automated mode
• Alignment mode
• Project mode
Which one you use depends on the degrees of similarly, difficulty
I. Pre-generated models
Automated Mode
For cases where target-template similarly is sufficiently high to allow fully 
automated modeling (> 50%)
- Requires only AA sequence of template protein
- Automatically selects appropriate template based on
BLAST E value, “best” structures
Alignment mode: best place to start if no structure (real or hypothesized) exists!

Alignment Mode
- For cases where target-template lower (<< 50%), and 
where alignment is non-trivial
- Requires your alignment be in a standard format, must 
include the template sequence
-Often PDB entries are not identical to their parent 
sequence (His-tags, mutations, etc.)
>> program first matches up the crystal structure sequence to its 
sequence in the alignment
- Program then builds the model based on this alignment
-Modeling program may CHANGE the alignment!

Project Mode
- Allows user to “tweek” the alignment by 
hand  
These days: lots of proteins work in automated mode

Load Fly NPC2 model and 2 HKA
Need to align it!

Hide other chains:
Align two structures using Chimera
Select the correct 
structures to align 
(it will choose the 
chain)
Hit apply
To see the resulting sequence alignment from the structural alignment:

Match-Align is for extracting the aligned sequence AFTER you have already 
aligned the structures using Matchmaker
